# Quadratic Functions & Optimization

## Learning Objectives

By the end of this chapter, you will be able to:

- Identify the vertex of a quadratic function and interpret it in context.
- Complete the square to convert between forms of a quadratic.
- Graph a parabola from its equation.
- Set up and solve optimization problems using quadratic functions.

---

## Quadratic Form & the Vertex

*(Standard form, vertex form; interpreting the vertex as a max/min.)*

## Completing the Square

*(Method and worked examples.)*

### Concept Check: The Vertex Formula {-}

*(To be drafted.)*

## Graphing Parabolas

*(Sketching from vertex form; effect of the leading coefficient.)*

## Optimization Problems

*(Maximizing crop yield vs. fertilizer input; optimal harvest timing; runoff/spray trajectory.)*

### Concept Check: Interpreting Max/Min in Context {-}

*(To be drafted.)*

## Practice: Optimization Case Study

*(Minimize the cost of a containment structure, or similar.)*

## Skills Drills

### Completing the Square

*(To be drafted.)*

### Vertex-Finding

*(To be drafted.)*

---

## Midterm 1 Preview {-}

This chapter concludes the material covered on Midterm 1 (Chapters 1–4). See the Midterm 1 Review chapter in the back matter for practice materials.
